steps.title: Identify exactly what the student's claim requires
steps.content: The student claims the explanatory notes were included primarily as a marketing device to help sell the stand-alone volume. Supporting this claim requires evidence that shows why the publishers chose to include the notes — specifically, that their motivation was commercial rather than literary.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice, then confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘The text of the poem as published in the quarterly journal is not identical to the text of the poem published in the stand-alone volume.’: Changes to the poem itself say nothing about why the notes were added; this addresses the poem, not the notes’ marketing purpose. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Many critics believe that the poet’s explanatory notes remove certain ambiguities of the poem and make it less interesting as a result.’: Critics’ literary opinions about the notes’ effect do not show the publishers’ motivation for including them. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘The publishers of the stand-alone volume requested the explanatory notes from the poet in order to make the book attractive to readers who already had a copy of the poem in a journal issue.’: Directly shows that the publishers sought the notes to attract buyers — a commercial, marketing purpose. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘Correspondence between the poet and the publisher reveals that the poet’s explanatory notes went through several drafts.’: Multiple drafts show the notes were revised, but give no evidence that the revision was driven by marketing goals rather than literary ones. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
